content.full_text:
BARTKIEWICZ, KRONICK & SHANAHAN
A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
1330 TWENTY-FIRST STREET, SUITE 200
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 446-4254
FACSIMILE (916) 446-4018

PAUL M. BARTKIEWICZ
STEPHEN A. KRONICK
RICHARD P. SHANAHAN
ALAN B. LILLY

August 23, 1991

Mr. Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
General Counsel
California Fair Political
Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Re: Request for an Advice Letter Concerning Mr. Edward
Addy, Director of Templeton Community Services
District

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     This office is general counsel to Templeton Community
Services District. The District is located in San Luis Obispo
County. I am writing on behalf of Mr. Edward Addy, a Director on
the Board of Directors of the Templeton Community Services
District. Mr. Addy desires an advice letter on whether he must
disqualify himself for making, participating in making, or using
his official position to influence a proposed arrangement between
the District and certain landowners concerning a District
groundwater development project. The proposed arrangement is
scheduled to be discussed at the District's September 16 meeting.
I understand the facts to be as follows:

     The Templeton Community Services District was formed
pursuant to the Community Services District Law found in Government
Code Sections 61000, et seq. Mr. Edward Addy became a member of
the Board of Directors of the Templeton Community Services District
on May 20, 1991.

     The District proposes to enter into an arrangement with
Mr. Chester E. and Vida L. Finley concerning groundwater
exploration and development on property which the Finleys own
located along El Ponar Road in San Luis Obispo County, California.
The property consists of approximately 180 acres. If the
groundwater exploration proves successful, the District would drill
production wells and pay the Finleys for the water produced. The
District would pay the Finleys approximately $40 for each acre foot
of water produced with annual minimum amount. The rate of payment
per acre foot would be adjusted annually pursuant to the California

Mr. Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
August 23, 1991
Page 2

Consumer Price Index and changes in District water rates. The
payment period is 30 years and payments over the 30 year period
could exceed a total of $1,000,000. The District also proposes to
acquire 4 acres of the Finley property at fair market value.

The proposal also provides that the District would
guarantee water service to any future subdivision of the Finley
property, provided that service to any such subdivision would not
involve more than 30 meters. Each lot purchaser would have to
comply with District rules and regulations governing water service,
including connection fees and water rates.

Mr. Addy has no financial interest whatever in the above-
referenced Finley land. He has no business relationship with
either Chester or Vida Finley.

Mr. Addy is a general partner in the partnership named
FYMA Associates. The other general partners are Robert Markwith,
Don Young and Bob Finley, the son of Chester and Vida Finley. Each
general partner has a 25% interest in the general partnership. The
only investment of the general partnership is in the land occupied
by the Main Street Grill Restaurant in Templeton, California.

Robert Markwith, Edward Addy and Young/Finley Corporation
are general partners in the general partnership named Main Street
Associates. Mr. Markwith and Mr. Addy each own a 25% interest in
that general partnership. Young/Finley Corporation owns a 50%
interest. Don Young and Bob Finley are the owners of the
Young/Finley Corporation. Main Street Associates owns and operates
the Main Street Grill. It pays rent on the restaurant building to
FYMA Associates. Bob Finley is President of Young/Finley
Corporation and is personally involved in the operation of the
restaurant.

Mr. Addy and Mr. Markwith loaned Young/Finley Corporation
$10,000 each. The loan is evidenced by a promissory note. An
option agreement was also executed whereby Mr. Markwith and Mr.
Addy have the right to convert the loan amounts to shares in the
Corporation at some future point. The option has not yet been
exercised. Young/Finley Corporation has financial interests in not
only the Main Street Grill, but also other restaurants in the San
Luis Obispo and Templeton areas.

By virtue of an agreement with his parents, Bob Finley
has a current beneficial interest in the property owned by his
parents which is the subject of the proposed arrangement between
the District and his parents, as referenced above. Bob Finley and
his family live on the property, and he operates the ranch.

Mr. Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
August 23, 1991
Page 3

     None of the businesses involving Mr. Addy and Bob Finley
has any financial interest in the property owned by Bob Finley's
parents.

     It would be most appreciated if you could advise Mr. Addy
whether he can participate in the discussions and decision
regarding the proposed arrangement between the District and Chester
and Vida Finley. As mentioned above, the matter is scheduled to
be on the agenda for the September 16, 1991, meeting of the Board
of Directors of the District. If you have any questions concerning
this matter, please feel free to contact me.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        STEPHEN A. KRONICK

3730/L3082291
cc:  Templeton Community Services District
     Mr. Edward Addy
